purpose: Discriminative premortem on plan + dispatch contract + investigation report (commit 4682054). Verify load-bearing claims at file:line before /autonomous dispatch.
verdict_summary: Plan is overwhelmingly accurate (all 4 reframes verified) and the investigation report is comprehensive. ONE HARD ELEPHANT discovered: the M3 res.send→HtmlOutput reclassification will break 2 currently-GREEN tests in crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs (reply.send + res.send assertions on TaintSinkType::FileWrite). The plan's pre-flight grep convention (res\.send.*path_traversal) is FILTER-BUGGED — those tests assert the sink_type FileWrite directly, not the projected vuln_type. Without expanding the M3 grep + updating the 2 tests in the same commit, M3's 'zero currently-GREEN tests transition RED' stop-threshold will FAIL.
verification_methodology: Read plan.md (756 lines), dispatch-contract.json (187 lines), investigation.json (388 lines). Ran cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --no-fail-fast --test vuln_migration_v1_red — recorded 33 FAILED test names. Read crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs lines 133-207 (enums), 1909-2070 (TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS), 2620-2728 (RUBY banks), 2836-2895 (SWIFT banks), 3445-3478 (OCAML_AST_SINKS). Read crates/tldr-core/src/security/ast_utils.rs lines 18-36 (call_node_kinds — Ruby = ['call', 'method_call'] no subshell). Read 7 fixtures: c/sql_injection_positive.c, cpp/command_injection_positive.cpp, swift/sql_injection_positive.swift, javascript/deserialization_positive.js (eval(d) — confirmed shape mismatch), typescript/deserialization_positive.ts (same), ruby/command_injection_positive.rb (`#{cmd}` backtick — confirmed subshell shape), elixir/sql_injection_positive.ex (Ecto.Adapters.SQL.query! — confirmed bang). Read crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs lines 140-280. Grepped fixtures + tests for FileWrite assertions on res.send/reply.send/response.send.

claim_1_red_count_33.result: VERIFIED — exact match: '133 passed; 33 failed' and 33 unique test names extracted
claim_1_red_count_33.discrepancy_with_brief: brief said 32; investigation explains the off-by-one as M3-CF-01 (32 entries) + M4-CF-01 (1 entry: python_xss_positive); 32+1=33 — CORRECT

claim_5_res_send_reclass_breaks_no_green.plan_claim: §8 Risk 1 mitigation: 'verified empirically at HEAD: typescript_path_traversal_positive uses fs.readFile not res.send; the only res.send test is the failing javascript_xss_positive itself'
claim_5_res_send_reclass_breaks_no_green.FINDING_HARD_ELEPHANT: INCORRECT — crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs has 5 FileWrite-asserting tests on the res/reply family, of which 2 will be broken by M3 reclassification:

tests_that_will_break.asserts: TaintSinkType::FileWrite for reply.send — plan moves to HtmlOutput
tests_that_will_break.outcome: WILL FLIP RED at M3 land

tests_that_will_break.asserts: TaintSinkType::FileWrite for res.send — plan moves to HtmlOutput
tests_that_will_break.outcome: WILL FLIP RED at M3 land

tests_that_stay_green.asserts: FileWrite on reply.redirect — plan KEEPS as FileWrite
FileWrite on reply.header — plan KEEPS as FileWrite
FileWrite on res.redirect — plan KEEPS as FileWrite

claim_5_res_send_reclass_breaks_no_green.root_cause_of_plan_miss: Plan's pre-flight grep convention (dispatch-contract M3 stop-threshold + plan §8 Risk 1) is `grep -rn 'res\.send.*path_traversal' crates/tldr-cli/tests/ crates/tldr-core/tests/`. This grep is filter-bugged because: (1) the rr_framework_integ_test.rs tests assert the sink_type variant `FileWrite` directly via matches!, NOT the projected vuln_type 'path_traversal'; (2) the plan's grep would return zero hits and falsely confirm safety. Plan's §8 Risk 1 mitigation explicitly claims 'the only res.send test is javascript_xss_positive itself' — empirically false.
claim_5_res_send_reclass_breaks_no_green.remediation: M1 deliverable MUST extend the pre-flight grep to: `grep -rEn 'res\.send|reply\.send|response\.send|Response.*send' crates/tldr-core/tests/ crates/tldr-cli/tests/` AND `grep -rE 'TaintSinkType::FileWrite' crates/tldr-core/tests/ crates/tldr-cli/tests/ | grep -B2 -A2 send`. Then either (a) update the 2 affected test assertions from FileWrite to HtmlOutput in the SAME commit as the M3 reclassification (atomic gate), OR (b) add a parallel HtmlOutput entry without removing the FileWrite entry (double-coverage). The plan currently chose option (a) framing ('move' the entries) but did not enumerate the test updates required. The dispatch-contract M3 stop-threshold 'zero GREEN tests transition RED' is therefore unachievable unless the M3 commit ALSO includes the rr_framework_integ_test.rs updates.
claim_5_res_send_reclass_breaks_no_green.result: BLOCKER-CLASS ELEPHANT — but easy to fix. M3 must ship the 2 test assertion updates atomic with the bank flip.

claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.plan_claim: JS/TS deserialization_positive fixtures use eval(d) which is wired as CodeEval/CommandInjection, not Deserialize
claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.javascript_fixture_content: export function handler(req, res, db) { const d = req.query.d; eval(d); }
claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.typescript_fixture_content: export function handler(req: any, res: any, db: any) { const d = req.query.d; eval(d); }
claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.current_eval_wiring: TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS L1910-1914: { call_names: ['eval'], sink_type: TaintSinkType::CodeEval }. vuln_type_from_sink projects CodeEval -> CommandInjection. Test asserts vuln_type='deserialization'.
claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.result: VERIFIED — fixture-shape mismatch confirmed. Plan's M4 fixture-fix proposal is correct.
claim_6_js_ts_eval_d_deser_fixture_shape.downstream_concern: Plan §8 Risk 4 verifies that javascript_deserialization_string_literal_fp.js currently contains the marker `eval(code)` and `res.send(html)` inside a string literal — read confirms this. After M4, the FP fixture remains valid because eval/res.send ARE still in the bank (eval as CodeEval; res.send post-M3 as HtmlOutput), and the FP fixture asserts ZERO findings from string-literal occurrences — that property is enforced by the canonical is_in_string filter, not by sink-bank composition. VERIFIED safe.

claim_7_ruby_backtick_subshell_carry_for.plan_claim: Ruby `` `#{cmd}` `` is parsed as subshell node, not call_expression; call_node_kinds(Ruby) doesn't include subshell; AST cannot structurally express the shape
claim_7_ruby_backtick_subshell_carry_for.extending_call_node_kinds_assessment: Adding 'subshell' to Ruby's call_node_kinds would broaden ALL Ruby sink-bank matching, not just ShellExec. RUBY_AST_SINKS has 7 banks (CodeEval, ShellExec, ShellExec/IO.popen, CodeEval-via-send-method, HtmlOutput-raw, FileOpen, HttpRequest, Deserialize). A subshell node has no function-name to extract — extract_call_name on subshell would likely return empty or panic. The downstream descent through banks would either (a) silently no-op (best case), or (b) match raw-fallback substring entries on backtick-containing text (FP risk). The plan's REJECTED option 2 ('extend call_node_kinds') is correctly rejected — but the rejection rationale ('would broaden matching across all Ruby sink banks unintentionally') is somewhat hand-wavy; a more rigorous rejection would be 'subshell nodes carry no call-name, so extract_call_name semantics break — this isn't an additive change, it's a control-flow extension'. Either way, the carry-forward decision is correct.
claim_7_ruby_backtick_subshell_carry_for.result: VERIFIED — carry-forward justified, mirrors FAI-v1 \bgets\b precedent.

claim_8_m2_m3_m4_file_disjoint.plan_claim: M2/M3/M4 are independently parallelizable after M1 lands
claim_8_m2_m3_m4_file_disjoint.FINDING_FILE_OVERLAP: M2, M3, M4 ALL edit crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs. M3 and M4 BOTH edit TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS specifically. The plan's Mermaid graph shows M2/M3/M4 in parallel after M1, but this is INCORRECT for parallel-agent execution — they cannot run as separate worktree branches without merge conflicts.
claim_8_m2_m3_m4_file_disjoint.FAI_v1_precedent: User's premortem brief explicitly cites this as 'Same trap as FAI-v1 / sanitizer-v1' — confirmed. The plan's own §2 line 115 acknowledges 'They land sequentially under one orchestrator but could be executed in parallel under a parallel-agent harness.' This claim is wrong for taint.rs. They MUST serialize (M2 → M3 → M4) under a single executor, OR use granular line-range coordination.
claim_8_m2_m3_m4_file_disjoint.remediation: Treat M2/M3/M4 as serialized within a single executor session. Plan's wave structure language ('parallel after M1') should be amended to 'serial after M1, in any order'. The dispatch-contract should not advertise 'parallelizable' to the orchestrator. RECOMMENDATION: orchestrator must execute M2 → M3 → M4 in sequence; parallel-agent harness is OUT-OF-SCOPE for this plan.
claim_8_m2_m3_m4_file_disjoint.result: ELEPHANT — plan's parallelism framing is misleading. Mitigation: serial execution under single executor.

claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.verification_method: Compare M3-CF-01.failing_tests_by_language list (sum) + M4-CF-01.python_xss_positive against the empirical 33
claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.M3_CF_01_count: Reading M3-carry-forward.json failing_tests_by_language: python(3) + typescript(2) + javascript(2) + java(2) + rust(4) + c(1) + cpp(4) + ruby(2) + swift(3) + csharp(4) + scala(2) + elixir(3) + lua(1) + luau(1) + ocaml(1) = 35 tests at M3 time
claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.M4_closure: M4 closed python_path_traversal_positive + python_deserialization_positive (2 of 3 Python). Net: 35 - 2 = 33. M4-CF-01 retains python_xss_positive.
claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.math_check: 33 RED = 33 (M3-CF-01 minus 2 Python closures) + 1 M4-CF-01 (python_xss) — wait, that's 33 + 1 = 34, off by one
claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.math_recompute: M3-CF-01 had 35 (per its own report). M4 closed 2 (python path_traversal, python deser). Remaining from M3-CF-01: 33. M4-CF-01 = python_xss_positive WAS already in M3-CF-01's python: ['python_xss_positive', 'python_path_traversal_positive', 'python_deserialization_positive']. So M4-CF-01 is NOT a new 34th carry-forward — it's the residual unhealed python entry from M3-CF-01. Therefore: M3-CF-01 minus 2 closed = 33. That IS the 33 RED count at HEAD. M4-CF-01 just rebrands the 1 remaining python entry. The closes_carry_forward field in the dispatch contract is CORRECT (closing M3-CF-01's residual + M4-CF-01's restated python entry = same 33 set).
claim_9_closes_carry_forward_completely.result: VERIFIED — math reconciles. 33 RED = 32 from M3-CF-01 (other-language) + 1 from M3-CF-01-python-xss (also restated as M4-CF-01).

claim_11_ruby_subshell_inexpressibility_.verification_method: Confirm subshell IS the tree-sitter-ruby node kind for backtick literals; check whether a non-call AST traversal handler could be added without the FP risk
claim_11_ruby_subshell_inexpressibility_.tree_sitter_ruby_subshell_node_kind: PRESUMED — investigation report and plan both name the kind as 'subshell'. tldr binary's tree-sitter-ruby version is the standard tree-sitter-ruby grammar where backtick command literals ARE parsed as `subshell` nodes (per upstream tree-sitter-ruby grammar.js). Cannot be re-verified at this premortem level without compiling and parsing — but documentation-level evidence is consistent.
claim_11_ruby_subshell_inexpressibility_.minimal_extension_assessment: A future taint-engine extension could add a dedicated detect_subshell_sinks_ast handler that walks subshell nodes and emits ShellExec-typed sinks with the interpolation-content as the receiver. This would NOT require call_node_kinds extension; it'd be a parallel detector. Out of scope for this milestone (engine extension, not bank addition).
claim_11_ruby_subshell_inexpressibility_.result: VERIFIED — plan's carry-forward classification is correct; the engine-extension path is correctly noted as future work.

elephants.title: M3 reclassification breaks 2 GREEN tests — plan's pre-flight grep is filter-bugged
elephants.evidence: crates/tldr-core/tests/rr_framework_integ_test.rs:157-177 (fastify_reply_send_reflected_via_compute_taint) and lines 237-257 (nestjs_res_send_reflected_via_compute_taint) both assert `matches!(s.sink_type, TaintSinkType::FileWrite)` for `reply.send` and `res.send` respectively. M3 plan moves these from FileWrite to HtmlOutput. The 2 tests will flip RED at M3 land.
elephants.remediation_required: M3 must ship as ATOMIC commit including (a) the bank flip in TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS, AND (b) the assertion update in rr_framework_integ_test.rs lines 168 + 248 from `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` to `TaintSinkType::HtmlOutput`. The pre-flight grep convention must be expanded to: `grep -rEn '(reply|res|response|Response)\.(send)\b' crates/tldr-core/tests/ crates/tldr-cli/tests/` AND `grep -rE 'TaintSinkType::FileWrite' crates/tldr-core/tests/ | grep -B5 -A5 'send'`. Plan §8 Risk 1 mitigation claim 'the only res.send test is javascript_xss_positive itself' is empirically false — must be retracted.
elephants.estimated_fix_effort: 5 minutes (2 line edits in test file, atomic with the bank flip commit). Trivial mechanically, just must not be missed.

elephants.title: M2/M3/M4 cannot truly parallelize — all edit taint.rs
elephants.evidence: M2 files_modified = ['crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs']. M3 files_modified = ['crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs (TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS only)']. M4 files_modified includes 'crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs (TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS Deserialize bank only)'. M3 and M4 both edit TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS. M2 edits multiple banks.
elephants.plan_section_to_amend: §2 Mermaid graph (M2/M3/M4 parallel framing); §2 line 115 'They land sequentially under one orchestrator but could be executed in parallel under a parallel-agent harness' — second clause is incorrect for taint.rs file; remove or qualify
elephants.remediation_required: Orchestrator must execute M2 → M3 → M4 in serial under a single executor session. Parallel-agent harness with separate worktrees would produce merge conflicts. The plan's wave structure should be amended to 'serial after M1, in any order'.
elephants.user_brief_alignment: The user's premortem brief explicitly flagged 'Same trap as FAI-v1 / sanitizer-v1' — confirmed.

elephants.title: Plan §1 sink-addition count is undercount: '~14 sink additions' actual is ~22
elephants.evidence: Counting sink AstSinkPattern entries proposed in plan §3: C(1) + Cpp(2) + CSharp(4) + Elixir(3) + Java(2) + Lua(1) + OCaml(1) + Python(1) + Ruby(1) + Scala(2) + Swift(3) + JS/TS(1 in M4 + 1 reclass via M3) ~= 22 distinct AstSinkPattern entries. Plan §1 says '~14 sink additions' and dispatch-contract M2.loc_delta_estimate says '+60 to +90 LOC (~14 new AstSinkPattern entries)'.
elephants.plan_section_to_amend: §1 per-language scope table totals; dispatch-contract M2.loc_delta_estimate
elephants.remediation_required: LOC estimate may need bump to +80 to +110 LOC. Not a blocker because actual entries-per-LOC ratio depends on member_patterns array sizes.
elephants.operational_impact: If the executor uses the LOC estimate as a stop-threshold (e.g., 'flag if delta exceeds estimate by >50%'), this could trigger a false alarm. Recommend amending the contract estimate.

tigers.title: Rust 4 RED tests source-extraction may be a structural engine refactor (not additive-fixable)
tigers.evidence: Investigation §rust_command_injection_positive: 'tree-sitter-rust parses the chained call as nested call_expression nodes; the canonical detect_sinks_ast walks all descendants. Command::new(...) is the FIRST call in the chain — the descendant matching ('', 'Command::new') is the inner call_expression whose text is std::process::Command::new("sh") — does NOT contain &cmd.' Plan §M1 acknowledges this and provides categorical decision-frame (a/b/c).
tigers.plan_section_to_amend: Plan correctly handles this — §M1 produces explicit categorical decision; §5 provides carry-forward path; dispatch-contract validator_mandate `rust_disposition_explicit` and `carry_forward_max_5` mandate cap.
tigers.outcome_paths: If M1 categorizes all 4 Rust as type-(b) [carry-forward], M5 reports 161/166 GREEN (28 closed of 32 originally targeted other-lang RED + 1 Ruby carry-forward + 4 Rust carry-forward = 5 carry-forward total, within the cap_max_5).
tigers.no_remediation_needed: Plan's M1 is the gating decision; risk is acknowledged + bounded.

tigers.title: java_deserialization_positive's flow may need engine-level taint propagation through .getBytes() chain
tigers.evidence: Investigation §java_deserialization_positive: 'The wildcard *.readObject SHOULD fire — but it does not in the empirical run. ROOT CAUSE: ... source variable d is not propagated through `d.getBytes()` because that's a method call on d, and the engine's variable-flow tracking may treat `d.getBytes()` as a sanitized/distinct value.'
tigers.plan_handling: Plan's M2 sink-bank addition for ('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(') is a band-aid: the raw-fallback substring path matches the OUTER call_expression text, which DOES contain `d` as a sub-token. If the substring path fires correctly, java_deserialization_positive transitions GREEN. If not, this becomes a Rust-class structural issue (taint propagation through method chains).
tigers.outcome_paths: If sink-bank addition fixes it: M2 includes java_deserialization_positive in transitions. If not: similar to Rust, becomes a carry-forward — but unaccounted-for in §5 carry-forward enumeration. M5 stop-threshold 'aggregate carry-forward count <= 5' would still pass (1 Ruby + max 4 Rust + 1 Java = 6 — EXCEEDS cap).
tigers.remediation_required: Plan §5 carry-forward enumeration should explicitly anticipate Java as a possible carry-forward in addition to Rust 4. The carry_forward_max_5 mandate should be amended to carry_forward_max_6 OR an explicit 'M1 verification gate' added — if M1 finds Java is non-additive-resolvable, surface as a carry-forward exception with rationale. The plan currently bounds the carry-forward to 5 (1 Ruby + 4 Rust); Java surfaces as a 6th in worst case.

carry_forward_cap_check.investigation_evidence: investigation.json §java_deserialization_positive flags 'EXPERIMENTAL VERIFICATION DEFERRED to M1 of vuln-source-parity-v1' and identifies a possible source-flow propagation issue similar to Rust
carry_forward_cap_check.potential_overflow: If M1 surfaces Java as non-additive-resolvable, total carry-forward = 6 (1 Ruby + 4 Rust + 1 Java)
carry_forward_cap_check.remediation: Either amend cap to 6 OR add explicit M1 mandate to surface Java as additive-fix-required (e.g., propose ('', 'new java.io.ObjectInputStream(') raw-fallback as the band-aid). Plan §3 Java sink addition lists this entry — if it fires, Java is closed; if not, carry-forward.

additional_observations.obs: Plan §1 success criteria says '165/166 passing' but stop-threshold §M5 says '>= 165/166'. Internally consistent but the dispatch-contract success_summary_post_m6.vuln_migration_v1_red says '165/166 passing (99.4%); +32 vs HEAD's 133/166'. These align with: 33 RED → 32 GREEN + 1 carry-forward. If M1 categorizes Rust 4 as type-(b), the count becomes 161/166 (97.0%); +28. Plan §M1 anticipates this branch correctly.
additional_observations.verdict: OK — math is internally consistent across plan sections.

additional_observations.obs: The dispatch-contract.staging_method.verification command has a subtle regex bug: `git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!vuln-source-parity-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK`. POSIX grep -E does NOT support negative lookahead `(?!...)`. The command would be a no-op — grep -E would interpret it as a literal string and find no matches. To make this work, either use `grep -P` (Perl regex) or `grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/' | grep -v 'vuln-source-parity-v1-plan'`.
additional_observations.verdict: MINOR — staging verification command is broken on macOS (BSD grep). Recommend amending to: `git show HEAD --stat | grep 'continuum/autonomous/' | grep -v 'vuln-source-parity-v1-plan' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK`

additional_observations.obs: Plan §3 for CSharp ShellExec proposes `member_patterns: &[('', 'System.Diagnostics.Process.Start'), ('', 'Process.Start(')]` — note the second entry has `(` in the field name, which would be interpreted as part of the substring match. This is intentional (raw-fallback substring path) but inconsistent with the other CSharp entries. Should be either both with `(` or both without.
additional_observations.verdict: MINOR — implementation detail; executor should normalize.

additional_observations.obs: Plan §3 Lua entry uses `("", ":query(")` — empty receiver + leading-colon field. This relies on the substring path matching against the call_expression text. The convention is consistent with how Lua's colon-method shape is handled. Not a duplication concern.

Plan does NOT explicitly address 'what happens to the 5 GREEN-pre-M3 path_traversal_string_literal_fp.{js,ts} fixtures that mention `res.send(html)` in a string literal'. The fixtures (verified: javascript/path_traversal_string_literal_fp.js + typescript counterpart) are FP-class — they assert ZERO findings from string-literal occurrences. After M3, res.send is wired as HtmlOutput; the FP fixture's string-literal occurrence still fires the canonical is_in_string filter, returning zero findings. Property preserved. Verified safe.

discriminative_findings_summary.verdict: CONDITIONAL-PASS — all 4 reframes verified accurate; ONE discoverable hard elephant (rr_framework_integ_test.rs FileWrite breakage) requires same-commit M3 fix before stop-threshold passes; M2/M3/M4 parallelism framing operationally misleading but workable in serial execution; LOC estimate undercount is informational

remediations_required_before_dispatch.change: Expand the pre-flight grep regex from `res\.send.*path_traversal` to `(reply|res|response|Response)\.(send)\b` AND `TaintSinkType::FileWrite.*\b(send|reply)`. Add to M3 stop-threshold: 'M3 commit MUST atomically include rr_framework_integ_test.rs:168 + :248 assertion updates from FileWrite to HtmlOutput.' Update §8 Risk 1 mitigation to remove the false claim 'the only res.send test is javascript_xss_positive itself' and replace with the explicit enumeration of the 2 affected rr_framework_integ_test.rs tests.
remediations_required_before_dispatch.estimate: 10 min plan edit + 2 LOC test edit during M3

remediations_required_before_dispatch.change: Remove the 'parallel under a parallel-agent harness' claim. Document M2/M3/M4 as serial under single executor. Mermaid graph could be amended to show M2 → M3 → M4 sequence after M1.

Bump cap to carry_forward_max_6 OR add an explicit M1 mandate to verify java_deserialization_positive's resolution path (additive vs. carry-forward). If additive, java is included in M2. If carry-forward, count becomes 6 in worst case.

Replace negative-lookahead PCRE regex with portable grep + grep -v pipeline: `git show HEAD --stat | grep 'continuum/autonomous/' | grep -v 'vuln-source-parity-v1-plan' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK`

conditions: RM-1 (blocker) — apply before /autonomous dispatch OR ensure M1 pre-flight grep is expanded to catch the rr_framework_integ_test.rs cases AND M3 milestone explicitly includes the 2 test assertion updates atomic with the bank flip
RM-2 (recommended) — orchestrator must serialize M2 → M3 → M4 under a single executor; do NOT delegate to parallel-agent harness for taint.rs edits
RM-3 (recommended) — anticipate Java carry-forward as a 6th if M1's diagnosis is non-additive
RM-4 (minor) — fix staging_method.verification before any M-report commit
go_no_go.without_remediation: BLOCK — M3's stop-threshold 'zero GREEN tests transition RED' will fail and require either commit revert or scope amendment mid-milestone
go_no_go.with_remediation_RM-1_only: CONDITIONAL-GO — minimum viable; orchestrator should verify M3 commit includes the 2 test edits before promoting to M5

premortem_metadata.investigation_duration: thorough — read all 3 plan documents, 2 carry-forward reports, 7 fixtures, 6 source-code line-ranges, ran cargo test for empirical RED count + extracted unique language list
